mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the monthly decline of PAYEMS (fewer net job losses over consecutive months) signals that labor market contraction is bottoming, which forward-looking equity markets price in as improving corporate earnings prospects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156923K reflecting sustained monthly job losses through early 2009
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS change acceleration and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following deceleration months are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rebounds by more than 10% from a multi-decade cyclical trough over a rolling 3-month window, this signals stabilization in residential construction demand, a historically leading indicator of broader economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 10% over trailing 3 months from a cyclical low
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1358K units near historic post-WWII lows as of March 2009
failure_condition: Following triggered HOUST rebounds, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where FEDFUNDS is held near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL shows subdued or declining inflation, the absence of near-term tightening risk lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy / disinflationary regime
failure_condition: During periods of near-zero FEDFUNDS and low CPI inflation, SPX 20-day forward returns underperform the full-sample unconditional mean, or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month decline in INDPRO decelerates for two consecutive months after a sharp downturn, it signals that industrial output contraction is losing momentum, prompting a short-horizon positive repricing of cyclical equities within the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline shrinks for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.6115 following steep monthly declines through late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following two consecutive months of decelerating INDPRO decline are not positive in a statistically significant majority (>55%) of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration where PAYEMS continues falling month-over-month while UNRATE simultaneously rises above its trailing 6-month average indicates an accelerating, self-reinforcing labor market downturn that depresses consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue outlooks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this reinforcing dynamic.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9 with rising trend alongside continued PAYEMS declines
failure_condition: Instances of joint PAYEMS decline and above-trend UNRATE rise are followed by non-negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS month-over-month growth turns positive after six or more consecutive months of decline, it signals a consumer spending inflection that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth turns positive after 6+ months of decline
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 704954 million dollars after a prolonged multi-month contraction into 2009
failure_condition: Following the RSAFS inflection trigger, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p>0.05) across historical trigger occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 registers a sharp quarterly contraction while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously held near the effective lower bound, the combination signals both maximal policy accommodation and a trough in aggregate output, jointly conditioning markets to anticipate a policy-driven recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. This mechanism requires the joint condition; GDP contraction alone without accommodative policy would not generate the same signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized contraction exceeds 2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 contracting sharply through late 2008/early 2009 amid aggressive Fed easing
regime_hint: post-shock recovery under zero rate policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following joint GDP contraction and near-zero FEDFUNDS conditions are negative or statistically indistinguishable from baseline in more than half of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Once UNRATE crosses above a critical threshold of 8.5% during a recessionary regime, the unemployment rate is understood by markets as a lagging indicator nearing its peak, and equities begin pricing in the eventual recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days despite the still-elevated unemployment level.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9 in snapshot but rapidly trending upward through the 2008-2009 recession toward multi-decade highs
failure_condition: Following UNRATE crossing above 8.5%, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or indistinguishable from baseline in more than 50% of historical occurrences across comparable recessions.
